rationale: The citing paper’s core contribution is implementing and testing an **incentive-aligned** UDE procedure in two experiments, and the cited paper’s main contribution is introducing and validating **incentive-aligned conjoint** with real-consequence designs. This strong construct and design overlap (incentive alignment in conjoint-style elicitation/prediction) makes the citation plausibly methodological/foundational rather than a casual mention, even though only a DOI-level match is available.
